chief_complaint: A 67-year-old man presented with a rare presentation of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC) encapsulated by IgG4-related autoimmune pancreatitis (AIP).
history: A 67-year-old man with IgG4-AIP underwent two 18 F-FAPI-42 PET/CT visualizations. The first PET/CT showed diffuse and intense FAPI uptake in the pancreas. PET/CT follow-up after a period of glucocorticoid treatment suggested focal and intense FAPI uptake along the pancreatic body.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: 18 F-FAPI-42 PET/CT scans showing diffuse and intense FAPI uptake in the pancreas, and focal and intense FAPI uptake along the pancreatic body after glucocorticoid treatment.
correct_diagnosis: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC) encapsulated by IgG4-related autoimmune pancreatitis (AIP)
differentials: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC) encapsulated by IgG4-related autoimmune pancreatitis (AIP)
correct_ranking: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC) encapsulated by IgG4-related autoimmune pancreatitis (AIP)
